title: PSA: Siri Can Turn on Night Shift on Apple Watch
content:
The Apple Watch has a hidden feature for activating Night Shift on the device, a Reddit user discovered recently. If you ask Siri on the Apple Watch to turn on ‚ÄåNight Shift‚Äå, ‚ÄåSiri‚Äå will do so, and the display will take on the dimmer, yellow hue that lowers blue light exposure.


It is not clear how long ‚ÄåSiri‚Äå has been able to activate ‚ÄåNight Shift‚Äå on the Apple Watch, but it seems to be recent. Apple has no documentation on ‚ÄåNight Shift‚Äå on Apple Watch, and it was not previously a watchOS 10 option. It's possible ‚ÄåNight Shift‚Äå is tied to watchOS 11, but it is not limited to devices running ‚ÄåwatchOS 11‚Äå. We were able to use the feature on an Apple Watch Series 9 with ‚ÄåwatchOS 10‚Äå installed.

Activating ‚ÄåNight Shift‚Äå can only be done through ‚ÄåSiri‚Äå, and there is no toggle in the Apple Watch Settings app or elsewhere on the device. ‚ÄåSiri‚Äå mentions scheduling ‚ÄåNight Shift‚Äå in the Settings app, but that's not an option at the current time in ‚ÄåwatchOS 10‚Äå or ‚ÄåwatchOS 11‚Äå.

‚ÄåNight Shift‚Äå does not appear to be available on all Apple Watch models, with Reddit users unable to activate it on a Series 5 and lower.

The option to suddenly turn on ‚ÄåNight Shift‚Äå without an accompanying setting suggests that it is a feature that has been accidentally introduced, and the toggle could be introduced in a future version of ‚ÄåwatchOS 11‚Äå or an upcoming ‚ÄåwatchOS 10‚Äå update.
This article, "PSA: Siri Can Turn on Night Shift on Apple Watch" first appeared on MacRumors.com
